questions.question: Find the shortest distance between the curve y = x^2 and the line x - 2y + 3 = 0, within an interval of 8 hours. (Assume the curve starts at (0, 0) and moves along the x-axis in the positive direction within the given interval).
questions.hint: Consider the point of tangency between the curve and the line

questions.question: A particle moves along a straight line with a constant acceleration of 2 m/s^2. If the particle covers a distance of 8 km in 8 hours, find the initial velocity of the particle. (Use vector calculus to solve the problem).
questions.hint: Use the equation of motion to relate distance, velocity, and acceleration

questions.question: A rigid body is rotating at a constant angular acceleration of α rad/s^2. If the body covers an arc length of 8 km in 8 hours, find the initial angular velocity of the body. (Use vector calculus to solve the problem).
questions.hint: Use the equation of motion to relate arc length, angular velocity, and angular acceleration
